evals.prompt: I just finished the PRD for team billing. Can you define the domain terms before we start the technical design? The PRD is in plans/team-billing-prd.md
evals.expected_output: Should find and read the PRD in plans/team-billing-prd.md. Should check for a matching scope document. Should explore the codebase for existing naming of billing-related concepts (subscriptions, plans, invoices, etc.). Should produce a glossary table with terms, definitions, aliases, code names, and status. Should flag ambiguities — e.g., does 'plan' mean a billing plan or a project plan? Should note naming conventions from the codebase. Should save to plans/team-billing-glossary.md. Should NOT modify the PRD file. Should NOT re-interview the user. Should end by suggesting '/design-feature'.

evals.prompt: ubiquitous-language — the PRD uses 'workspace' and 'project' interchangeably and the code calls it 'org'. Can you sort this out before design?
evals.expected_output: Should find the PRD in plans/, read it fully, and specifically flag the workspace/project/org ambiguity. Should check the codebase to determine which term is most established. Should propose one canonical term with rationale (user-facing consistency, codebase momentum, or precision). Should include an Ambiguities Resolved section documenting this specific conflict and the recommended resolution. Should extract all other domain terms from the PRD as well, not just the ones the user mentioned. Should save the glossary and append Pipeline Status to the PRD.

evals.prompt: Let's harden the terminology for the dashboard sharing feature before we design it. I want to make sure we're all using the same words.
evals.expected_output: Should find the dashboard sharing PRD in plans/. Should extract all domain terms including sharing-related concepts (viewer, editor, share link, permissions, access levels). Should check the codebase for existing auth/permissions terminology and flag any divergence from the PRD's language. Should note whether the codebase uses 'role' vs 'permission' vs 'access level' and recommend one. Should produce a complete glossary with naming conventions section. Should ask a clarifying question only if a genuine ambiguity cannot be resolved from the PRD and code alone.
